label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this problem. So, there's a teacher with four students, each gets a different number from a set S of four consecutive two-digit integers. The teacher told them that some number is divisible by 6 and another different one is divisible by 7. Then all students said "no" when asked if they could figure out S. But after hearing that, each could determine S. We need to find the sum of all possible greatest elements of S. First, let me note down the key points: - S is four consecutive two-digit integers. So numbers like 10,11,12,13 or 15,16,17,18, etc. - Each student has a different number from S. - The set S contains at least one multiple of 6 and one multiple of 7. Importantly, they are different numbers. - All four students initially say they can't
